name: Crab Combination Soup
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Fresh or thawed frozen Crabmeat
1/2 cup Drained whole or sliced Bamboo shoots (from a 8 oz can)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place mushrooms in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Let stand for 30 minutes. Drain. Remove and discard the stems. Cut the caps into thin slices.
2. Flake the crabmeat. Rinse the scallops with water, drain, and cut into thin slices. Cut the bamboo shoots into thin strips. Chop the green onions.
3. Heat oil in a small omelet or crepe pan. Add eggs and tilt the pan so that the egg completely covers the bottom. Cook over medium-high heat until the egg is set. Loosen the edges, turn the omelet over to cook the other side, remove from the pan, roll up, and cut into thin strips.
4. Pour broth into a 3-quart saucepan. Cook over high heat until it boils. Stir in mushrooms, crabmeat, scallops, bamboo shoots, onions, sliced egg, ginger, and pepper. Return soup to boil.
5. Combine cornstarch with 4 tablespoons of water, rice wine (or dry sherry), and soy sauce. Stir this mixture into the soup. Return the soup to a boil.
6. Beat the remaining egg whites and 2 tablespoons of water. Slowly drizzle the egg whites into the soup while stirring vigorously.
